title: bromus vulgaris subsp. robustus
intro: Bromus vulgaris subsp. robustus, also known as robust meadow brome, is a subspecies of the common meadow brome plant, a type of grass native to western North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its essential oils and phenolic compounds. The robust meadow brome has been reported to have health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems, including coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
